content: We can reframe sequential decision-making in Soft Q-Learning as traversing a temporal event graph, where each state-action pair is an edge and each visitation to a state after a transition is a unique node, aligning with Soft Q-Learning's maximum entropy objective to explore all possible temporal trajectories instead of just converging to a single optimal path. This formulation addresses the underutilization of Soft Q-Learning in modern RL by leveraging the temporal event graph's lossless, compact representation of all interaction histories to efficiently store and compute the maximum entropy policy's partition function.
